guest: Sam Rattner, founder and investor, age 27

business_ideas.idea: Water taxi service for lakes and river destinations
Vending machine business with custom sourcing strategy
Riverboat casino conversions for entertainment venues
Showroom AI-powered shopping platform for premium brands

predictions.text: Uber will acquire or build water taxi services by 2028
Showroom will reach $1B valuation within 5 years

dynamics: Rattner shares extensive due diligence stories on various business ideas. Sam asks clarifying questions and is impressed by depth of research and execution. Episode highlights Rattner's philosophy on spotting overlooked opportunities.
frameworks: Never pitch twice - if investor needs to be sold twice, they didn't want it
Potential you see in others is mirror of what you would do if you were them
The value is in the duck calls - focus on niche ecosystems most don't know about
Make 5x improvements not 10% improvements - look for step-function opportunities
learnings: Best business opportunities often in industries others overlook as unsexy
Due diligence requires talking to users and stakeholders, not just desk research
Capital requirements and regulatory barriers create defensive moats
Transaction-based platforms more defensible than pure audience/attention plays
People reveal true values through repeated actions and small decisions

banger_quotes.quote: The value is in the duck calls
Never pitch twice - if I need to sell it again, they didn't want it
Take people at face value for what they are today
Have you tried just solving the problem by solving the problem

numbers_dropped: Sold sports betting company for $40 million at age 23
High school snow removal business: $50-60K per winter
Water taxi boat earning: $400K per summer, 3 boats = $1.2M annually
Vending machine company: 5000 machines, $1-1.3M annual cash flow
Showroom: thousands of merchants, tens of millions of SKUs
notable: Unique perspective on finding overlooked regional and niche business opportunities; focus on practical due diligence over theory
